commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Needs maintainer API/security-boundary acceptance and contributor real-behavior proof; there is no narrow automated code repair to queue.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain defect was found; the new sensitive hook surface is gated by the existing conversation-access policy. (sha=5d827c1034e5779b7f9753ca900ce042035b4b9f)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `model_failover` and `model_failure_terminal` typed plugin hooks, hook runner methods, embedded/auto-reply emission paths, docs, and focused regression tests.

PR surface: Source +228, Tests +485, Docs +19, Generated 0. Total +732 across 20 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a feature PR. Current main source inspection shows the requested hook names are absent, while the PR’s tests exercise mocked runtime branches rather than a failing current-main bug reproduction.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs maintainer API/security-boundary acceptance and contributor real-behavior proof; there is no narrow automated code repair to queue.

target.title: feat(plugins): add model failover and terminal failure hooks
target.body:
## Summary

Adds two plugin observability hooks for model failure handling:

- `model_failover`: fires from the embedded-runner failover decision path with failed source refs, selected target refs, stage, decision, reason metadata, recoverability, timeout/abort/status flags, and fallback availability.
- `model_failure_terminal`: fires when a before-reply model run reaches terminal failure after all configured attempts are exhausted.

This PR does not change fallback policy, default user-facing failure copy, provider retry behavior, or context re-injection behavior.

## Cleanup in this revision

- Rebased the PR branch onto current `origin/main` as a single feature commit.
- Resolved the `resolve_exec_env` hook conflict in `src/plugins/hooks.ts` by preserving both independent hook additions.
- Regenerated `docs/.generated/config-baseline.sha256` on the rebased tree.
- Fixed the terminal payload issue: `model_failure_terminal.finalMessage` now emits the resolved delivered failure reply, not the raw `formatErrorMessage(err)` fallback summary.
- Fixed the selected-target issue in the normal gateway/auto-reply path: `runAgentTurnWithFallback` now opts into `exposeNextCandidateToRun` and forwards `runOptions.nextCandidate` into `runEmbeddedAgent` as `nextModelFallbackCandidate`.
- Removed the unrelated Anthropic API-key `cacheRetention` default change from this PR surface.
- Added regressions covering both the terminal failure payload and the auto-reply selected fallback target wiring.

## Security / compatibility

- New capability: plugin observability hooks.
- No new network calls.
- No token/secret handling changes.
- No command/tool execution surface changes.
- `model_failure_terminal` remains conversation-access gated for non-bundled plugins because it can expose user-facing failure copy.
- Attempt summaries expose provider/model/reason metadata, not raw provider error blobs.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Normal channel auto-reply fallback should expose the selected fallback target to the embedded runner so `model_failover` does not report the failed source as the selected target. Terminal failure hooks should receive the resolved delivered failure reply, while structured `attempts` retain provider/model/reason details.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS Darwin 25.3.0 arm64, Node `v25.6.0`, pnpm `11.2.2`, PR head `5d827c1034e5779b7f9753ca900ce042035b4b9f`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```bash
pnpm vitest --config test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply.config.ts run src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts -t "forwards selected fallback candidates|surfaces billing guidance for mixed-cause" --reporter verbose
```

- **Evidence after fix**: Terminal output from the patched source checkout showed the two targeted auto-reply runtime branch regressions passing:

```text
✓ |auto-reply| src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts > runAgentTurnWithFallback > forwards selected fallback candidates into embedded auto-reply runs 76ms
✓ |auto-reply| src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts > runAgentTurnWithFallback > surfaces billing guidance for mixed-cause fallback exhaustion 2ms

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  2 passed | 154 skipped (156)
```

- **Observed result after fix**: The selected-target regression exercises the `runAgentTurnWithFallback` auto-reply path, verifies `runWithModelFallback` is called with `exposeNextCandidateToRun: true`, passes a selected candidate through the fallback callback, and asserts `runEmbeddedAgent` receives `{ provider: "openai", model: "gpt-5.4" }` as `nextModelFallbackCandidate`. The terminal regression exercises the same before-reply terminal branch and asserts the hook receives `finalMessage: "billing"` while attempts still include `anthropic/claude` as `rate_limit` and `openai/gpt-5.4` as `billing`.
- **What was not tested**: I did not force a live external provider outage or run a networked end-to-end model-failover incident. The proof uses mocked model runtimes inside the real auto-reply fallback branch so the hook payload wiring is deterministic and redacted.

## Validation

Environment:

- macOS Darwin 25.3.0 arm64
- Node `v25.6.0`
- pnpm `11.2.2`
- Head `5d827c1034e5779b7f9753ca900ce042035b4b9f`

Commands run on the patched tree:

```bash
node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts src/plugins/hooks.model-failover.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/failover-observation.model-failover-hook.test.ts src/plugins/loader.test.ts
pnpm config:docs:check
pnpm vitest --config test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply.config.ts run src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts -t "forwards selected fallback candidates|surfaces billing guidance for mixed-cause" --reporter verbose
npx oxfmt --check src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts extensions/anthropic/config-defaults.ts
pnpm tsgo:test:src
pnpm lint --threads=8
git diff --check origin/main...HEAD
```

Observed results:

- Focused Vitest command passed 4 shards:
  - bundled: 152 tests passed
  - auto-reply: 156 tests passed
  - agents: 8 tests passed
  - plugins: 12 tests passed
- Targeted verbose auto-reply proof passed 2 selected tests and skipped the remaining 154 tests in that file.
- `pnpm config:docs:check`: `OK docs/.generated/config-baseline.sha256`
- `oxfmt --check`: all matched files use the correct format
- `pnpm tsgo:test:src`: passed
- `pnpm lint --threads=8`: passed core, extensions, and scripts oxlint shards
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`: no whitespace/conflict-marker findings

## Linked issue

Closes #70976
Related #65824
